summarization.a: Schematic illustrating the reactant pulse stage, where fluorine- and hydrogen-containing species approach the Hf/Zr–O surface. Downward arrows indicate adsorption-driven surface interaction prior to any temperature-dependent reaction.
summarization.b: Conceptual depiction of a self-limiting surface reaction occurring below the indicated temperature thresholds (<657 K for HfO₂ and <534 K for ZrO₂). Adsorbed species modify the surface without net material removal, consistent with saturation-limited behavior.
summarization.c: Schematic of the etch regime activated above the temperature thresholds (>657 K for HfO₂ and >534 K for ZrO₂). Upward arrows indicate desorption of reaction products, corresponding to active material removal from the surface.

a.question: What four types of atoms are identified in the legend for this reaction schematic?
What is the first stage of the process shown in the schematic in this subfigure?
a.answer: The first stage is the reactant pulse, where incoming species approach the Hf/Zr–O surface.

c.question: Does the etch reaction shown in this panel occur under the same temperature conditions as the preceding self-limiting reaction stage?
To achieve active etching of HfO₂, must the process temperature be maintained above or below 657 K?
